informal_statement.text: Given a directed graph with non-negative integer edge weights, a source vertex, and a vertex count n, return a vector dist of length n where dist[v] is the length of the shortest path from source to v, or None if no such path exists.
preconditions: every edge's endpoints are in [0, n)
required_properties: for every v, if dist[v] == Some(d), there exists a path from source to v of total weight d
for every v, if dist[v] == Some(d), no path from source to v has total weight less than d
for every v, if dist[v] == None, there is no path from source to v

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: every edge's endpoints lie in [0, n) and every weight is non-negative.
Size: the returned dist vector has length n.
Achievability: if dist[v] == Some(d), there exists a path from source to v with total weight d.
Optimality: if dist[v] == Some(d), no path from source to v has total weight less than d.
common_failure_modes: stating only a weaker existence claim
asserting only on the shortest-path tree edges

semantic_units.description: Unreachability: if dist[v] == None, there is no path from source to v.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (n source : Nat) (g : Graph), (dijkstra n source g).length = n
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned distance table has length n.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (n source : Nat) (g : Graph), source < n → (dijkstra n source g).get? source = some (some 0)
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The distance from the source to itself is zero.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (n source v : Nat) (g : Graph) (d : Nat), NonNegativeWeights g → (dijkstra n source g).get? v = some (some d) → ∀ d', PathWeight g source v d' → d ≤ d'
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When every edge weight is non-negative, if dist[v] == Some(d) then no path from source to v has weight less than d.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (n source v : Nat) (g : Graph) (d : Nat), (dijkstra n source g).get? v = some (some d) → PathWeight g source v d
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] == Some(d), there exists a path from source to v with total weight d.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (n source v : Nat) (g : Graph), (dijkstra n source g).get? v = some none → ¬ ∃ d, PathWeight g source v d
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] == None, there is no path from source to v.

generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned distance vector has length n (one entry per vertex).
Each entry is an optional distance: Some d if a path exists, otherwise None.
The distance from the source to itself is 0.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem none_implies_no_path (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) (hV : g.Vcard = n) :
  let dist := dijkstra_ref g w s n;
  ∀ v : Fin n,
    dist[v] = none → ¬ (∃ p : Path g s v, p.lengthCost w < ∞)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns None for a vertex, then there is no path from the source to that vertex.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem some_implies_path (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) (hV : g.Vcard = n) :
  let dist := dijkstra_ref g w s n;
  ∀ v : Fin n,
    dist[v] = some d → ∃ p : Path g s v, p.lengthCost w = d
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns Some d for a vertex, then there exists a path from the source to that vertex whose total weight is exactly d.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem upper_bound (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) (hV : g.Vcard = n) :
  let dist := dijkstra_ref g w s n;
  ∀ v : Fin n, ∀ p : Path g s v,
    dist[v].elim (fun _ => True) (fun d => d ≤ p.lengthCost w)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: For any reachable vertex v and any path p from s to v, the computed distance is ≤ the cost of that path (so it is an upper bound on the shortest path).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem optimality (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) (hV : g.Vcard = n) :
  let dist := dijkstra_ref g w s n;
  ∀ v : Fin n,
    dist[v].elim (fun _ => True)
      (fun d => ∀ d' : Nat,
        (∃ p : Path g s v, p.lengthCost w = d') → d ≤ d' ∧ (d' = d → True))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Optimality: when a distance is returned as Some d, it is minimal among all path costs to v.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem relaxation_invariant (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) :
  let dist := dijkstra_ref g w s n;
  ∀ e : g.Edge,
    let u := e.src; let v := e.dst;
    dist[⟨u.1, _⟩].elim (fun _ => True)
      (fun du =>
        dist[⟨v.1, _⟩].elim (fun _ => True)
          (fun dv => dv ≤ du + w e))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Triangle inequality / relaxation property: for every edge u→v, if distances are defined then dv ≤ du + weight(u,v).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem nonneg_weights_required (w : g.Edge → Nat) :
  ∀ e : g.Edge, 0 ≤ w e
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Edge weights are non-negative (Nat), which is the key precondition for Dijkstra correctness.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem algorithm_terminates (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) :
  ∃ t : Nat, dijkstra_ref_steps g w s n = t
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The reference implementation terminates (e.g., due to finite vertex set and bounded priority-queue operations).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem correctness_matches_spec (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) (hV : g.Vcard = n) :
  let dist := dijkstra_ref g w s n;
  ∀ v : Fin n,
    dist[v] = shortestPathCostOption g w s v
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The computed vector matches the mathematical shortest-path cost function (Some shortest cost, or None if unreachable).
